result:
Here's a summary of the changes made:

## Problem

New user accounts now get the `dailyprompt` tag subscribed by default. This means when a new user opens the Reader for the first time, `getFollowedTagsUseCase.get()` returns a non-empty list (containing at least the `dailyprompt` tag). The onboarding check `userTags.isEmpty()` evaluates to `false`, causing the Welcome/onboarding screen to be skipped entirely.

## Solution

Modified two files to treat users who only have blogging-prompts-related tags (`dailyprompt`, `dailyprompt-*`, `bloganuary`) as if they have no user-chosen tags, thus showing the onboarding flow:

### 1. `ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt` (line 153-157)

Added a check `hasOnlyBloggingPromptsTags` that evaluates to `true` when all followed tags are blogging prompt tags. The onboarding empty state is now shown when `userTags.isEmpty() || hasOnlyBloggingPromptsTags`.

### 2. `ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt` (line 96-99)

Same logic applied to `checkAndLoadInterests()` - the interests picker (tag selection screen) is now shown when the user only has blogging prompt tags, instead of immediately closing.

Both changes use the existing constants from `BloggingPromptsPostTagProvider` (`BLOGGING_PROMPT_TAG = "dailyprompt"` and `BLOGANUARY_TAG = "bloganuary"`) to identify blogging prompt tags via `startsWith("dailyprompt")` (which covers both `dailyprompt` and `dailyprompt-*` variants) and exact match on `bloganuary`.
